debug_info.goal:
k : ℕ
hk : 2 ≤ k
⊢ OeisA080170.GCDCondition k ↔ OeisA080170.PrimePowerCondition k

failed_attempts:
constructor
  · intro h
    -- The implication from the GCD condition to the prime power condition
    -- corresponds to the known result that if the GCD is 1, then k is not
    -- dominated by any single prime power (i.e., k > P^2).
    sorry
  · intro h
    -- The reverse implication establishes that if k is sufficiently composite
    -- relative to its prime power factors, the GCD of the binomials is 1.
    sorry

-- This conjecture is a known theorem by Ramharter (1983).
  -- The condition gcd(binom(2k,k), ..., binom((k+1)k,k)) = 1 holds if and only if
  -- k is not dominated by any of its prime power factors (k > P^2).
  -- Formalizing the full number-theoretic proof is beyond the scope of this block.
  sorry
